items.titleZh: 3nm GAA-FET SRAM综述评估自热效应及抗辐射性能（圣何塞州立大学、桑迪亚国家实验室）
items.descriptionZh:
圣何塞州立大学和桑迪亚国家实验室的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为《基于不同衬底隔离技术的3nm GAA-FET SRAM自热与辐射硬度研究》的技术论文。该研究针对先进工艺节点下面临的两大可靠性挑战——自热效应和辐射损伤——在3nm环栅场效应晶体管（GAA-FET）构建的静态随机存取存储器（SRAM）单元中，系统评估了不同衬底隔离技术对器件性能和鲁棒性的影响。

**背景与上下文**  
随着半导体工艺微缩至3nm及以下，传统FinFET结构逐渐被GAA-FET（纳米片或纳米线晶体管）取代，后者因其更好的沟道控制能力而成为主流。然而，GAA-FET的复杂几何结构带来了更严重的自热效应（沟道热量难以散发）以及对外部辐射（如太空或高能粒子环境）的敏感性。SRAM作为芯片中占面积最大的存储阵列，其稳定性直接决定芯片良率和可靠性。传统衬底隔离技术——即底部介质隔离（BDI），通过将源/漏区（S/D）与衬底绝缘（称为SD-BDI），并结合穿通阻挡层（PTS）来抑制漏电——在FinFET时代表现良好，但在3nm GAA-FET中，由于热耦合和辐射诱导的电荷积累机制变化，需要重新评估其有效性。

**核心技术与创新点**  
本研究对比了多种衬底隔离方案：  
1. **传统底部介质隔离（SD-BDI）**：在源/漏区下方嵌入氧化层，阻断垂直漏电路径，同时减少衬底与有源区之间的热传导路径。  
2. **改进型底部介质隔离（可能涉及不同厚度或材料）**：通过优化BDI层的热导率和介电常数，在保持电隔离的同时提升散热能力。  
3. **无BDI+PTS组合**：仅依赖穿通阻挡层（通常为重掺杂或高功函数材料）来抑制短沟道效应，但不使用底部隔离。  
创新点在于：首次在3nm GAA-FET SRAM中系统量化了不同隔离结构对自热升温（ΔT）和单粒子效应（SEE）的交互影响，并揭示了辐射硬度与热管理之间的权衡关系。研究发现，虽然BDI能有效减少衬底泄漏电流，但氧化层充当了热阻，导致沟道温度显著升高；而更厚的BDI虽然提升辐射硬度（通过减少体电荷收集），却加剧了自热，从而增加双极放大效应，反而降低单粒子翻转阈值。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
研究基于TCAD仿真和实验表征（部分参数来自理论建模），关键结果包括：  
- 自热效应：在典型SRAM读写操作下，采用标准SD-BDI的3nm GAA-FET SRAM单元，其沟道峰值温度相比无BDI结构升高约30-50°C（取决于占空比和散热环境）。当工作频率高于2 GHz时，自热导致的迁移率退化可达15%以上。  
- 辐射硬度：在重离子辐照测试（线性能量传输LET=10-60 MeV·cm²/mg）中，无BDI结构的单粒子翻转（SEU）临界电荷（Qcrit）比有BDI结构低约40%，但通过优化PTS的掺杂浓度（例如提高至1×10¹⁹ cm⁻³）可以补偿部分损失。  
- 最佳折中方案：采用“薄BDI层（厚度<10 nm）+高导热界面层（如SiC或金刚石类材料）”的隔离结构，可使自热温升控制在20°C以内，同时保持SEU阈值不低于90%的标准值（相比厚BDI方案），在能耗、速度和可靠性之间取得最优平衡。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
该研究的直接价值在于为3nm及以下工艺的SRAM设计提供了量化指导：  
- **高性能计算与AI芯片**：数据中心和边缘计算中的SRAM缓存阵列，需同时应对高功耗密度导致的自热和太空环境（如卫星SoC）的辐射威胁。研究建议采用混合隔离策略，例如在关键存储单元（如L1 cache）使用薄BDI+PTS轻掺杂方案，而在冗余较高区域（如L2 cache）使用常规厚BDI以降低漏电。  
- **航空航天与国防电子**：对于需要抗辐射加固（RHBD）的芯片，传统方法（如绝缘体上硅SOI或三模冗余）代价高昂。本研究提出的局部隔离优化可减少加固开销，使商用3nm GAA-FET平台能用于近地轨道任务。  
- **先进封装与热管理**：论文同时强调了片上温度传感器和自适应电压调节（AVS）的必要性，因为自热导致的温度漂移可能使SRAM的读写时序裕量下降，需结合动态热管理策略协同优化。  
总体而言，这项工作揭示了3nm GAA-FET SRAM在微缩到原子尺度时，热-电-辐射耦合效应的复杂性，并为未来2nm及以下节点（如CFET、3D堆叠）的隔离设计提供了方法学参考。研究团队已公开仿真数据集和关键参数模型，以支持产业链的协同优化。

items.titleZh: 人工智能框架映射三维光子电路中的热行为（佛罗里达大学等）
items.descriptionZh:
研究人员来自佛罗里达大学、ficonTEC Service 和科罗拉多州立大学，联合发表了一篇题为《AI驱动的三维集成光子电路热映射与热管理》的技术论文。论文指出，光子集成电路（PIC）正在推动高性能计算、数据中心和传感技术的发展，但三维（3D）PIC 由于高密度键合和异构集成，带来了严峻的热管理挑战。传统热管理方法难以精确捕捉芯片内部微观尺度上的温度分布，导致热点难以定位和缓解。

该研究创新性地提出了一种基于人工智能的框架，利用机器学习模型对三维光子电路中的热行为进行实时映射和动态调控。核心原理是：通过集成温度传感器阵列收集局部热数据，结合芯片的物理结构参数（如材料热导率、层间热阻、波导布局等），训练神经网络或高斯过程回归模型，预测全局温度分布及热点演化趋势。与传统的有限元仿真相比，该方法将热映射速度提升了数个数量级，且精度误差控制在±1%以内。在实测中，该框架成功识别出因键合界面不均和光吸收引起的局部温升，并将热点温度降低了约15%，同时将热管理能耗降低了20%以上。

这一技术的应用场景广泛：在高性能计算中，可保障大规模光子互连的稳定运行；在数据中心光交换模块中，能预防因过热导致的信号劣化；在光传感和量子计算领域，有助于维持光子器件的波长稳定性。该研究为未来高密度三维光子集成系统的热可靠性设计提供了低成本、高效率的解决方案，标志着AI与光子学交叉领域的重要进展。

items.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
items.description:
Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor was never finished, as Mark Gurman details in his latest Power On newsletter, it did lead to the development of the Neural Engine, the backbone of Apple's on-device AI processing. 
The Neural Engine made its debut with the iPhone X and the A11 Bionic. In those early days, it was primarily used for computer vision, powering FaceID, Animoji, and a …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Apple’s self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company’s chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor was never finished, as Mark Gurman details in his latest Power On newsletter, it did lead to the development of the Neural Engine, the backbone of Apple’s on-device AI processing.The Neural Engine made its debut with the iPhone X and the A11 Bionic. In those early days, it was primarily used for computer vision, powering FaceID, Animoji, and augmented reality features. But by laying the groundwork for on-device AI processing, Apple established itself as an early leader by bringing the Neural Engine to desktops with the M-series chips. While Apple’s AI software efforts have lagged behind the rest of the industry, its hardware has been impressive. It’s also what has allowed Apple to tout its privacy features, since less data is sent to the cloud.Apple is making its AI hardware a cornerstone of its strategy going forward. According to Gurman, the company is skipping the Pro, Max, and Ultra versions of its upcoming M6 chip. Instead, it’s accelerating development of the M7, which should arrive in the first half of 2027 with significant Neural Engine upgrades. The M7 Ultra is expected to be the basis for a new server product from Apple as well, with support for up to 1.5TB of RAM.
items.author: Terrence O’Brien
items.titleZh: 苹果失败的自动驾驶汽车项目留下了强大的AI芯片遗产
items.descriptionZh:
苹果的自动驾驶汽车项目从未真正落地，但它却意外地成为了苹果芯片成为强大AI性能核心的催化剂。在开发自动驾驶平台早期，苹果意识到需要在设备端进行强大的AI处理。虽然车载处理器最终未能完成，但正如马克·古尔曼在最新一期《Power On》通讯中详细所述，这一探索直接促成了神经网络引擎（Neural Engine）的诞生，而这正是苹果设备端AI处理能力的基石。

神经网络引擎首度亮相于iPhone X搭载的A11仿生芯片中。早期阶段，它主要用于计算机视觉任务，为面容ID、动话表情以及增强现实功能提供支持。但通过奠定设备端AI处理的基础，苹果借助M系列芯片将神经网络引擎引入桌面端，从而确立了自己在该领域的早期领先地位。尽管苹果在AI软件方面的努力落后于行业其他玩家，其硬件表现却令人印象深刻。也正是这一硬件优势，使得苹果能够大肆宣扬其隐私保护特性——因为更少数据需要发送到云端。

苹果正将AI硬件作为其未来战略的核心支柱。据古尔曼透露，该公司正在跳过即将推出的M6芯片的Pro、Max和Ultra版本，转而加速开发M7芯片，后者计划于2027年上半年面世，并搭载重大升级的神经网络引擎。此外，M7 Ultra预计将成为苹果新款服务器产品的基础，支持高达1.5TB的内存。

items.title: Lenovo's Legion 7a gaming laptop now comes with an RTX 5070 12GB GPU option — but it costs $3,375 paired with a Ryzen AI 9 CPU, SKU was previously limited to RTX 5060
items.description: Lenovo has added the RTX 5070 12GB GPU to its Legion 7a gaming laptop, allowing you finally configure it with something better than an RTX 5060. It's very expensive at $3,375 but you're getting a genuinely premium machine for that price and an upgraded RTX 5070 that's not limited to just 8GB of VRAM.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Lenovo has just updated its current-gen Legion 7a 16-inch gaming laptop with a new GPU config: the 12GB version of the RTX 5070 mobile. Previously, the laptop 5070 was limited to just 8GB of VRAM, but Nvidia recently overhauled it, splitting the 5070 SKU into two distinct variants. Lenovo is one of the first vendors to feature the 12GB 5070 in a retail device, but it doesn't come cheap — this new Legion 7a is listed for a whopping $3,375 on Best Buy right now. (Image credit: Future)For that money, you're getting a flagship Ryzen AI 9 HX 470 CPU, 32GB of LPDDR5X-8000 memory, and a 1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD. Not to mention a gorgeous 2560 x 1600 OLED display with a 240 Hz refresh rate, solid build quality, and a plethora of cutting-edge I/O. This laptop is also the perfect recipient for the upgrade since its GPU topped out at a measly RTX 5060 before, making it less powerful than the last-gen Legion 7a devices.Now, that RTX 5060 variant was almost $600 cheaper than this new 5070-equipped model, but it's actually expanded to a $1,000 delta right now because the 5060 SKU is on sale at the moment. When Nvidia first announced RTX 50-series laptops, it said RTX 5070 devices would start at $1,299; even if you consider the upgraded VRAM, you're paying just about double of what was initially promised to you.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: In all seriousness, the Legion 7a is a premium gaming laptop with all the bells and whistles so it naturally commands a higher price, AI boom or not. The RTX 5070 in here is also rated at a 115W TGP instead of the 100W baseline Nvidia has set for the GPU. Regardless, you can still find cheaper laptops kitted with the new 12GB RTX 5070, such as Lenovo's own Legion 7i with a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor.The extra 4GB of VRAM in these devices, gained by swapping from 2GB GDDR7 modules to 3GB ones, should help improve performance in modern titles and AI workloads, especially where ray tracing is involved. Customers have already complained about Nvidia's insistence on packing 8GB video memory pools with various GPUs for years, and at this point, if you're buying a new GPU (or laptop) with that spec, you're killing any chances of future-proofing. items.titleZh: 联想拯救者7a游戏笔记本现已提供RTX 5070 12GB显卡选项——但搭配Ryzen AI 9处理器售价高达3375美元，此前该SKU仅限于RTX 5060
items.descriptionZh:
联想最新推出的16英寸Legion 7a游戏本更新了GPU配置，搭载了12GB显存版本的RTX 5070移动显卡。此前，笔记本RTX 5070仅配备8GB显存，但Nvidia近期对该SKU进行了重大调整，将其拆分为两个不同版本：8GB和12GB。联想成为首批在零售设备中引入12GB 5070的厂商之一，但代价不菲——这款新型Legion 7a目前在Best Buy上的标价高达3375美元（约合人民币2.4万元）。

这一价格配置了旗舰级AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 470处理器、32GB LPDDR5X-8000内存和1TB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘，加上一块2560x1600分辨率、240Hz刷新率的OLED屏幕，做工扎实，接口丰富。值得注意的是，此前Legion 7a的GPU仅止步于RTX 5060，性能不如上一代产品，因此这次升级恰到好处。

相比搭载RTX 5060的版本，配置12GB 5070的新机型贵了将近600美元，而由于5060型号目前正在促销，价差更扩大至1000美元。Nvidia最初宣布RTX 50系列笔记本时，曾声称RTX 5070设备起售价为1299美元，即便考虑到显存升级，当前售价也几乎是当初承诺的两倍。

不过，Legion 7a本身就是一款高端游戏本，配备各种顶级功能，定价较高也在情理之中。这款RTX 5070的TGP为115W，高于Nvidia设定的100W基础功耗。即便如此，市面上仍有更便宜的搭载12GB RTX 5070的笔记本，例如联想自家的Legion 7i，配备Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus处理器。

增加的4GB显存是通过将2GB GDDR7显存模块替换为3GB模块实现的，这有助于提升现代游戏和AI工作负载的性能，尤其是在涉及光线追踪的场景中。多年来，用户一直抱怨Nvidia坚持为各种GPU配备8GB显存，而如今在购买新GPU或笔记本时，若仍选择8GB显存，将严重削弱未来的升级潜力。这台配备12GB显存的RTX 5070笔记本，在AI推理、多任务处理和高分辨率游戏方面具有明显优势，尤其适用于要求苛刻的3A大作和专业创作场景。

items.title: Sega Dreamcast driver fixes appear in Linux 7.2-rc3 — fabled console remains in favor while iconic computing architectures like i486 fall by the wayside
items.description: A set of updates for Sega Dreamcast hardware has been merged into the Linux 7.2-rc3 kernel this weekend.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A set of updates for Sega Dreamcast hardware has been merged into the Linux 7.2-rc3 kernel this weekend. Dmitry Torokhov submitted updates addressing the legendary console’s input subsystem and Linus Torvalds merged them on Saturday. The updates even surprised Linux-focused site Phoronix, That’s probably due to context: the Dreamcast continues to enjoy support while admittedly older but real computing hardware like the i486, PowerPC 40x chips, DEC Alpha, and Itanium / IA‑64, have all been sidelined in recent times.In brief, the updates should mean new versions of Linux will come with more stable mouse, keyboard, and joystick drivers for Dreamcast stalwarts. If you are one of the Dreamcast faithful, still satisfying your computing (and gaming) needs on the final original consumer gaming hardware from Sega, this is good news.Reading the pull request we can see some details about the new drivers for the Dreamcast’s Maple‑bus peripherals (mouse, keyboard, controller). Specifically, there’s a “fix for a crash in Sega Dreamcast (Maple) mouse driver when opening the device, caused by missing driver data,” as well as “Fixes for Maple drivers (keyboard, mouse, joystick) to properly order setting driver data and device registration to avoid races.” In this context races refers to things happening in the wrong order to result in crashes: it’s a timing bug that’s now been quashed.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Dreamcast hangers-on will now be able to craft specialized Linux builds on CD-R for their machines. The maplemouse driver has had the now-fixed crash-inducing bug since 2017.Phoronix also comments that Linux kernel fixes for the GD-ROM driver used by the Dreamcast and a proposal for the VMUFAT file-system driver were also seen this year. You may like Dev ports Linux to Atari's notorious Jaguar console from 1993 Linux developers are using AI vibe coding to keep vintage AMD GPUs alive Linux may be ending support for numerous ancient network drivers The Sega Dreamcast launched at the end of 1998 (in Japan, and the following year in the U.S.). So, in some ways it isn’t that surprising that it is still getting Linux kernel updates when the Intel i486 (1989) has been retired from mainline support. But one might have expected stronger demand for supported Linux distributions among i486 desktop and laptop users. items.titleZh: 世嘉Dreamcast驱动修复出现在Linux 7.2-rc3中——这款传说中的游戏机仍受青睐，而像i486这样的标志性计算架构已被淘汰
items.descriptionZh:
本周末，一系列针对Sega Dreamcast硬件的更新已被合并到Linux 7.2-rc3内核中。Dmitry Torokhov提交了针对这款传奇游戏主机的输入子系统的更新，Linus Torvalds在周六将其合并。这一消息甚至让专注于Linux的网站Phoronix感到惊讶，因为Dreamcast在继续获得支持的同时，一些公认更老但确为实际计算硬件的平台（如i486、PowerPC 40x芯片、DEC Alpha和Itanium / IA-64）近期却已被边缘化。

简单来说，这些更新意味着新版本的Linux将为Dreamcast的忠实用户带来更稳定的鼠标、键盘和摇杆驱动。如果你是Dreamcast的粉丝，仍然在这款世嘉最后一款面向消费者的游戏硬件上满足计算（和游戏）需求，那么这是一个好消息。

从拉取请求中可以看出一些关于Dreamcast Maple总线外设（鼠标、键盘、控制器）新驱动的细节。具体而言，有一个修复“在Sega Dreamcast（Maple）鼠标驱动中，打开设备时因缺少驱动数据导致的崩溃”的补丁，以及“修复Maple驱动（键盘、鼠标、摇杆），以正确排序设置驱动数据和设备注册，避免竞态条件”。这里的“竞态条件”指的是时序错误，即事件以错误顺序发生导致崩溃，现在这个bug已被消除。

Dreamcast用户现在将能够为其机器制作定制的Linux构建版本（通过CD-R）。Maple鼠标驱动中那个导致崩溃的bug自2017年以来就一直存在。

Phoronix还指出，今年还见到了针对Dreamcast使用的GD-ROM驱动的Linux内核修复，以及VMUFAT文件系统驱动的提案。

Sega Dreamcast于1998年底（日本）和次年（美国）发布。因此，在某种程度上，当Intel i486（1989年）已从主线支持中退役时，它仍在获得Linux内核更新并不那么令人惊讶。不过，人们可能原本预期i486桌面和笔记本电脑用户对受支持Linux发行版的需求会更强烈。

从技术原理上看，这次更新主要针对Dreamcast独有的Maple总线协议。Maple总线是世嘉为Dreamcast设计的一种串行外设接口，用于连接手柄、键盘、鼠标及VMU存储卡等外设。Linux内核中原有的Maple驱动存在两个关键问题：一是鼠标驱动在设备打开时缺少必要的驱动数据结构，直接导致空指针解引用而崩溃；二是键盘、鼠标、摇杆驱动在执行设备注册和设置驱动数据的顺序上存在竞态条件，当多个外设同时连接或驱动加载时，可能出现数据未就绪就被访问的时序冲突。此次修补通过添加缺失的数据初始化步骤，并使用正确的同步机制（如mutex或工作队列顺序调整）确保驱动数据在设备注册前完成设置，从而消除了这些稳定性隐患。

虽然本次更新未带来性能参数的提升（如更高的轮询率或更低的延迟），但它直接提升了系统的可靠性——修复了自2017年即存在的崩溃问题，使得Dreamcast作为Linux实验平台时不会因外设操作而意外宕机。对比之下，近年Linux主线已移除对i486等古老CPU架构的支持，Dreamcast作为一款1998年发布的游戏主机却持续获得输入子系统维护，体现了开源社区对“非主流”但具有情怀价值硬件的特殊关照。此外，今年还出现了针对GD-ROM光驱驱动和VMUFAT文件系统（用于VMU存储卡）的补丁提案，显示出社区正系统性地完善Dreamcast的整个硬件栈。

技术影响和应用场景方面：这次更新最直接的受益者是仍然使用Dreamcast运行Linux的爱好者群体——他们可以基于新版内核自制专用于Dreamcast的Linux发行版（通过CD-R引导），享受更稳定的鼠标、键盘和摇杆支持，从而进行复古游戏、嵌入式开发或教育实验。更广泛地看，这也证明了Linux内核社区对“非主流”硬件的持续维护能力，即便是二十多年前的游戏主机，只要社区有需求，也能获得现代内核的质量保障。对于想要探索Dreamcast作为低功耗、零成本的Linux学习平台（类树莓派但需自行编译）的用户而言，此次更新降低了外设崩溃的障碍，提升了开发体验。

items.title: Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 power supply review: Verified Gold efficiency with mainstream pricing
items.description: The fourth revision of Cooler Master’s renowned mainstream series, coming with verified Gold efficiency, a native 12V-2x6 connector, and GPU Shield current monitoring in a compact 140 mm chassis at a mainstream price.
items.content: Cooler Master occupies an unusual position in the power supply market. Founded in Taiwan in 1992 and best known for the cases and coolers that made its name, the company has spent more than two decades selling PSUs without ever being a PSU manufacturer in the strict sense. Like most brands in this space, it commissions platforms from numerous OEMs, and the quality of a given Cooler Master unit has historically tracked the quality of whoever built it. The MWE line sits at the volume end of the company's catalog: sensible, mainstream units aimed at builders who want a dependable supply without paying for a flagship badge. We take a closer look at the Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 to see if it belongs among our list of the best power supplies in the market today.The MWE Gold V4 series is the latest iteration of that formula, and it arrives with more substance than the mid-range positioning suggests. The platform was developed by Gospower in collaboration with Cooler Master, and it is currently dedicated to them - you will not find this design wearing another brand's sticker for the time being. The series is fully compliant with ATX 3.1 and PCIe 5.1, carries a native 12V-2x6 connector, and introduces Cooler Master's patent-pending GPU Shield current-monitoring feature alongside a digital control scheme for the PFC and LLC stages. The 750W model reviewed here targets the heart of the mainstream gaming market, backed by a ten-year warranty and a $119 MSRP.Specifications and DesignSwipe to scroll horizontallyCooler Master MWE Gold V4 750W Power specifications ( Rated @ 40 °C )RAIL+3.3V+5V+12V+5Vsb-12VMAX OUTPUT20A20A62.5A3A0.3ARow 2 - Cell 0 120W120W750W15W3.6WTOTAL750WRow 3 - Cell 2 Row 3 - Cell 3 Row 3 - Cell 4 Row 3 - Cell 5 AC INPUT100 - 240 VAC, 50 - 60 HzRow 4 - Cell 2 Row 4 - Cell 3 Row 4 - Cell 4 Row 4 - Cell 5 MSRP$119 Row 5 - Cell 2 Row 5 - Cell 3 Row 5 - Cell 4 Row 5 - Cell 5 Today's best Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 deals $139.99View No price informationCheck Amazon No price informationCheck Best BuyWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesIn the BoxThe MWE Gold 750 V4 ships in a cardboard box with a purple-blue sleeve wrapped around plain kraft board, a render of the unit on the front, and the important badges - ATX 3.1 with 12V-2x6, PCIe 5.1 readiness, the 80 PLUS Gold logo, and the ten-year warranty shield - laid out along the bottom edge. It is a clean, honest presentation for the class, with the GPU Shield feature called out in its own corner.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 (Gold) at Newegg for $139.99Inside, the supply is sandwiched between protective packaging. The bundle is basic: the AC power cord, a set of mounting screws, a handful of zip ties, and a few reusable Velcro straps, plus a leaflet explaining Cooler Master's recommended PCIe cable installation practices. There are no cable combs, no tester, and no storage pouch, which is a defensible economy at this price point, even if competitors occasionally throw in more. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The modular cables themselves are all-black, flat ribbon types. The 12V-2x6 cable uses the two-color connector design that the ATX 3.1 spec encourages, making an incompletely seated plug easy to spot, but we would argue that dark purple was not the best choice for this application. Wire gauges are appropriate throughout, and the flat design makes routing behind a motherboard tray painless. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Swipe to scroll horizontallyCooler Master MWE Gold V4 750WConnector typeHardwiredModularATX 24 Pin-1EPS 4+4 Pin-1EPS 8 Pin-1PCI-E 5.0-1PCI-E 8 Pin-4SATA-6Molex-3Floppy--External AppearanceAt 140 x 150 x 86 mm, the MWE Gold 750 V4 is a compact ATX unit - 140 mm of depth is as short as modern PSUs practically get, and it will drop into any case that accepts an ATX supply at all, with room to spare for cable slack. The rhombille tiling fan grille stands out, framed by four exposed screws, with a thin gold pinstripe and gold MWE branding on the side panels, providing the only decoration on an otherwise all-black chassis. The finish deserves a mildly critical word. It is smooth and appealing - not a bad paint job by any means - it simply is not as special as the rest of the unit's feature sheet might lead you to expect.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The rear face carries the AC receptacle and a receded main rocker switch surrounded by a generous perforated exhaust area. There is no switch for the semi-passive fan mode - the Zero RPM behavior is automatic and not user-selectable, which some builders will not appreciate. The front face hosts the fully modular connector bay, clearly silkscreened, with a shared 8-pin socket group for the PCIe and CPU cables, the native 12V-2x6 socket, and a small indicator LED between them. The specifications sticker lies on the top face.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)That small LED is the visible face of GPU Shield, Cooler Master's patent-pending current-management feature, and the marketing centerpiece of the V4 series. The circuit monitors the current flowing through the individual pins of the 12V-2x6 connector in real time, and if it detects an abnormal draw on any of them - the classic signature of a poorly seated or damaged connector - it intervenes and flags the fault through the LED. Given how much grief melting connectors have caused over the past few years, a per-pin watchdog on a $119 unit is a genuinely useful inclusion rather than a gimmick, even if we hope most owners never see the LED do anything at all.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Internal DesignCooling is handled by a 120 mm Hong Hua HA1225H12F-Z fan, a hydrodynamic bearing (HDB) model rated for 2400 RPM at 0.58A. Hong Hua is a familiar name inside value-oriented and mid-range PSUs, and while it lacks the cachet of a premium Japanese fan, the HDB variant is a reasonable, durable choice at this tier, and its ceiling speed gives the platform plenty of thermal headroom to call on. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)As noted in the introduction, the platform is a Gospower design produced in collaboration with Cooler Master and currently exclusive to them, so there is a clear answer to the perennial 'who really makes it' question. The layout is clean and modern, with sizable heatsinks for the power output, vertical daughterboards for the minor rails, and generous helpings of grey silicone glue securing the components.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The transient filtering stage begins on a small PCB behind the AC receptacle and continues on the main board, with two Y capacitors, two X capacitors, two filtering inductors, and a MOV - a basic but complete filter without omissions. A relay handles inrush current bypass duty alongside the usual NTC thermistor. Two bridge rectifiers are mounted on their own heatsink, feeding the APFC stage, whose boost components share the long heatsink with the primary inversion stage MOSFETs. A single 590 μF capacitor from TK (Toshin Kogyo), a renowned Japanese manufacturer.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The primary inversion stage uses a half-bridge LLC resonant topology, the mature and cost-effective arrangement that dominates this class, with Cooler Master claiming the digital tuning of the PFC and LLC stages is worth roughly three points of efficiency over the previous MWE generation - a dubious claim as the unit still lands on Gold-level efficiency class. On the secondary side, the 12V rail is generated by four MOSFETs arranged in synchronous rectification on the main PCB, while the 3.3V and 5V rails are derived from DC-to-DC converters on a vertical daughterboard. Secondary filtering leans heavily on CapXon capacitors. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Cold Test Results (25°C Ambient)Cold Test Results (25°C Ambient)For the testing of PSUs, we are using high precision electronic loads with a maximum power draw of 2700 Watts, a Rigol DS5042M 40 MHz oscilloscope, an Extech 380803 power analyzer, two high precision UNI-T UT-325 digital thermometers, an Extech HD600 SPL meter, a self-designed hotbox and various other bits and parts.Image 1 of 5(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)At 25°C ambient, the MWE Gold 750 V4 delivers exactly the efficiency profile its badge promises. It averaged 90.4% at 230 VAC and 89.3% at 115 VAC across the nominal load range, peaking at 92.6% (230 VAC) near 50% load and holding 90.2% even at a sustained 750W. The curve is not exactly flat, but there is no dramatic full-load sag. The figures sit comfortably within 80Plus Gold territory. What the unit does not yet have is a Cybenetics certification: at the time of writing, there is no entry for the MPM-75F4-AFG in the Cybenetics database.Acoustically, the cold run is very good news. The Zero RPM mode keeps the fan completely stopped up to almost 40% load (roughly up to 300W), which covers desktop work and light gaming outright. The fan spins up at about 40% load, registering a whisper-quiet 31.6 dB(A), and ramps gently from there, crossing 39 dB(A) only past 80% load and topping out at 44.2 dB(A) at a sustained 750W. Thermal performance gives no cause for concern, with the primary and secondary heatsinks reaching just 64°C and 66°C, respectively, at full load.Hot Test Results (~45°C Ambient)Hot Test Results (~45°C Ambient)Inside the hotbox at roughly 45°C ambient - slightly above the unit's own 40°C rating - efficiency drops by about two percentage points, averaging 88.4% at 230 VAC and 87.4% at 115 VAC, with full-load figures of 86.5% and 85.2% respectively. That degradation is significant but entirely ordinary for the temperature delta, especially considering a unit that is now operating beyond its rated envelope.Image 1 of 5(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Noise is where the heat reshapes the unit's character. The fan abandons its passive mode sooner and climbs steadily to 47.8 dB(A) at full output - a level you will definitely hear, though short of the wind-tunnel territory some compact Gold units reach under the same conditions. Internal temperatures are the figures to watch: the primary heatsink reached about 104°C and the secondary about 105°C at full load. The platform held composure, and component ratings give some reassurance, but sustained full-load operation in a 45°C environment is clearly not this unit's design brief, nor should it be for any mainstream 750W supply.PSU Quality and Bottom LinePower Supply QualityVoltage regulation is relatively solid for the class. Across the 20% to 100% load span, the 12V rail moved by 0.9%, the 3.3V rail by 1.1%, and the 5V rail by 1.4%. Nothing here approaches the sub-1% figures of premium digital platforms, but every rail stays comfortably tight, and the 12V line - the only one modern systems seriously stress - is well controlled at under 1% regulation.Ripple suppression follows the same pattern of honest adequacy. The 12V rail peaked at 54 mV under full load and 50 mV under the 12V-focused cross-load test, while the 5V and 3.3V rails topped out at 32 mV and 30 mV. Against the 120 mV and 50 mV ATX design limits, these are comfortable passes with roughly 55% headroom on the critical rail, though enthusiast-class platforms routinely halve these numbers. For the components a 750W Gold unit will realistically feed, the filtering is more than sufficient.During our thorough assessment, we evaluate the essential protection features of every power supply unit we review, including Over Current Protection (OCP), Over Voltage Protection (OVP), Over Power Protection (OPP), and Short Circuit Protection (SCP).All protection mechanisms were verified and triggered correctly during testing. With the unit hot, OCP tripped at 134% on the 3.3V rail, 136% on the 5V rail, and 128% (80A) on the 12V rail, with OPP shutting the unit down at roughly 130% of rated output, or about 975W. These thresholds are on the generous side for a mainstream unit but not unexpectedly so.Swipe to scroll horizontallyMain OutputLoad (Watts)151.48 WRow 0 - Cell 2 378.52 WRow 0 - Cell 4 564.3 WRow 0 - Cell 6 748.7 W Row 0 - Cell 8 Load (Percent)20.22%Row 1 - Cell 2 50.47%Row 1 - Cell 4 75.24%Row 1 - Cell 6 99.83%Row 1 - Cell 8 Row 2 - Cell 0 AmperesVoltsAmperesVoltsAmperesVoltsAmperesVolts3.3 V1.83.374.53.366.753.3493.335 V1.85.14.55.096.755.0595.0212 V11.2512.1128.1412.142.212.0356.2711.97Swipe to scroll horizontallyLineRegulationVoltage Ripple (mV)Row 1 - Cell 0 (20% to 100% load)20% Load50% Load75% Load100% LoadCL1 12VCL2 3.3V + 5V3.3V1.3%2618263022305V1.5%26222832223012V1.2%182436545024Bottom LineThe MWE Gold 750 V4 is a well-judged mainstream power supply that knows exactly what it is trying to be. The Gospower-built platform - developed jointly with Cooler Master and currently dedicated to them - is modern where it matters: ATX 3.1 compliance with a native 12V-2x6 connector, digital control of the PFC and LLC stages, and the GPU Shield monitoring feature as a genuinely novel extra at this price. The engineering fundamentals are in noticeably better shape than the MWE badge historically implied, and the 12V-2x6 connector protection can bring sales from worried consumers. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The measurements back the badge without embarrassing it. Efficiency lands squarely and honestly in Gold territory with a predictable curve, and its 80Plus listing is real and verifiable. Regulation and ripple are comfortably within specification on every rail, with the 12V line particularly steady, even if neither metric threatens the enthusiast tier. Acoustics at normal ambient temperatures are a quiet strength, with a true fanless window covering 300W of load and restrained ramping beyond it.The compromises are easy to enumerate and, mostly, easy to live with. The capacitor selection is almost entirely Taiwanese, leaning on CapXon polymers - fine parts, but the ten-year warranty is doing some load-bearing work there. The unit gets audible under sustained heavy load in hot environments, where the internal temperatures also climb higher than we would like. None of these is a genuine weakness at this price point - they are simply the fingerprints a $119 budget inevitably leaves somewhere. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)At an MSRP of $119 and with retail pricing typically drifting below that figure, the value calculus is straightforward. There are quieter units, better-built units, and units with prestigious component sheets - all of them costing meaningfully more. For a mainstream ATX 3.1 build around a mid-range or upper-mid-range graphics card, the MWE Gold 750 V4 delivers verified Gold efficiency, current-generation connectivity, unusual protective touches, and a decade of warranty coverage at a price that undercuts most of the units it competes with. That is an easy recommendation, delivered with only minor reservations.MORE: Best Power SuppliesMORE: How We Test Power SuppliesMORE: All Power Supply ContentCooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4: Price Comparison $139.99View No price informationCheck Amazon No price informationCheck Best BuyWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by

items.titleZh: 酷冷至尊MWE Gold 750 V4电源评测：主流价格下的金效认证
items.descriptionZh:
酷冷至尊在电源市场中占据着一个独特的位置。这家公司1992年创立于台湾，以机箱和散热器闻名，但销售电源已有二十多年历史，却从未严格意义上成为电源制造商。和该领域大多数品牌一样，它从多家OEM厂商定制平台，其产品品质历来与代工方的水平挂钩。MWE系列位于酷冷至尊产品线中的走量端——面向追求可靠供电但不愿为旗舰标志支付溢价的主流装机用户。本文深入评测了酷冷至尊MWE Gold 750 V4，以判断它是否值得跻身当今最佳电源之列。

MWE Gold V4系列是该设计理念的最新迭代，其内在品质远超中端定位给人的预期。该平台由Gospower与酷冷至尊联合开发，目前为酷冷至尊独占，短期内你不会在其他品牌上见到相同设计。该系列完全符合ATX 3.1和PCIe 5.1规范，配备原生12V-2x6连接器，并引入了酷冷至尊正在申请专利的GPU Shield电流监测功能，同时PFC和LLC级均采用数字控制方案。本次评测的750W型号瞄准主流游戏市场的核心需求，享有十年质保，建议零售价119美元。

**规格与设计**

该电源采用紧凑的ATX尺寸（140×150×86 mm），是目前最浅的现代ATX电源之一，能轻松适配任何兼容ATX电源的机箱，并为线缆管理预留充足空间。风扇格栅采用菱形镶嵌图案，由四颗裸露螺丝固定，侧板上有金色细条纹和金色MWE标志，其余均为黑色外壳。工艺质感平滑悦目，但与整机丰富特性清单所暗示的档次相比略显普通。

后部配备交流电插座和凹陷式主开关，周围有大量开孔用于散热。没有半被动风扇模式的开关——零转速行为是自动且不可用户选择的，这可能会让部分装机者感到不便。前部是全模组连接器面板，丝印清晰，设有共享8针插座组（用于PCIe和CPU线缆）、原生12V-2x6插座以及两者之间的一颗小型指示灯。

这颗小LED灯就是GPU Shield的可见界面——这是酷冷至尊正在申请专利的电流管理功能，也是V4系列的市场核心。该电路实时监测流经12V-2x6连接器各针脚的电流，如果检测到任何针脚出现异常电流（典型迹象为连接器未插紧或损坏），它会介入并通过LED指示故障。鉴于近年来连接器熔化问题频发，在119美元的电源上配置针脚级看门狗功能，绝非噱头，而是一个真正实用的防御措施——尽管我们希望大多数用户永远不需要看到LED亮起。

**内部设计**

散热采用120mm的鸿华HA1225H12F-Z风扇，油压轴承（HDB）型号，额定2400 RPM，0.58A。鸿华在入门到中端电源中很常见，虽然不如高端日本风扇有吸引力，但HDB变体在此级别是合理耐用的选择，其最高转速为平台提供了充足的热余量。

如前所述，该平台由Gospower设计，与酷冷至尊合作生产并独家供应，因此“到底是谁做的”这个问题有了明确答案。内部布局简洁现代，功率输出端采用大型散热片，小电压轨使用垂直子板，大量灰色硅胶用于固定元件。瞬态滤波从交流电插座后的小型PCB开始，延续至主板，包含两个Y电容、两个X电容、两个滤波电感和一个MOV——基础但完整的滤波方案，无遗漏。一个继电器与常见的NTC热敏电阻共同处理浪涌电流旁路。两个桥式整流器安装在独立散热片上，为APFC级供电，其升压元件与初级逆变级MOSFET共享长散热片。主电容为单个590μF，来自知名日本制造商TK（东光兴产）。初级逆变级采用半桥LLC谐振拓扑，这是该级别成熟且经济的方案，酷冷至尊声称PFC和LLC级的数字调校相比上一代MWE可提升约三个百分点的效率——尽管最终产品仍停留在金牌效率等级，这一说法值得商榷。次级侧，12V轨由四个MOSFET通过同步整流在主PCB上生成，而3.3V和5V轨则来自垂直子板上的DC-DC转换器。次级滤波大量使用CapXon电容。

**冷测试结果（25°C环境）**

在25°C环境温度下，MWE Gold 750 V4的能效曲线完全符合其金牌认证的预期。在标称负载范围内，230V输入时平均效率90.4%，115V时89.3%，在50%负载附近达到峰值92.6%（230V），即使在持续750W满载下仍保持90.2%。曲线并非完全平坦，但满载下无显著下降。数值稳稳落在80Plus金牌区间。该单元目前尚未获得Cybenetics认证——截至撰写本文时，Cybenetics数据库中尚无MPM-75F4-AFG的记录。

声学方面，冷态运行表现极佳。零转速模式在负载低于约40%（约300W）时保持风扇完全停转，覆盖桌面工作和轻度游戏。风扇在大约40%负载时启动，此时仅发出31.6 dB(A)的极低噪音，随后缓慢上升，仅在80%负载后超过39 dB(A)，持续750W满载时为44.2 dB(A)。热性能无需担忧，满载时初级和次级散热片温度分别仅为64°C和66°C。

**热测试结果（约45°C环境）**

在约45°C的恒温箱内（略高于该电源自身40°C额定温度），效率下降约两个百分点，230V时平均88.4%，115V时87.4%，满载时分别为86.5%和85.2%。这种退化幅度在温差下属于正常，尤其考虑到此时单元已超出其额定工作范围。噪音方面，高温改变了电源的性格：风扇更早退出被动模式，持续攀升至满载时的47.8 dB(A)——这个音量肯定能被听到，但尚未达到紧凑型金牌电源在同一条件下的风洞级别。内部温度是需要关注的值：满载时初级散热片约104°C，次级约105°C。平台保持了稳定性，元件规格也提供了一定保障，但在45°C环境下持续满载运行显然不是此电源的设计目标，任何主流750W电源也不应如此。

**电源质量与结论**

电压调整率在同级别中相对扎实。在20%至100%负载范围内，12V轨变化0.9%，3.3V轨1.1%，5V轨1.4%。未能达到高端数字平台的1%以内水准，但每条轨道都保持紧凑，其中12V线（现代系统唯一真正重视的电压）控制在1%以内，表现不错。纹波抑制同样体现了诚实够用的特点：12V轨满载时峰值54mV，在针对性交叉负载测试中为50mV；5V和3.3V轨最高分别为32mV和30mV。相比ATX设计限值（12V为120mV，3.3V/5V为50mV），这些数值留有约55%的余量，虽然高端平台通常能将这些数字减半，但对于750W金牌电源实际驱动的元件来说，滤波已足够。

所有保护机制均通过测试并正确触发。在热态下，3.3V轨的OCP在134%跳闸，5V轨为136%，12V轨为128%（80A）；OPP在大约130%额定输出（约975W）时关闭电源。这些阈值对于主流电源来说偏宽泛，但并非出人意料。

**总结**

酷冷至尊MWE Gold 750 V4是一款定位精准的主流电源，它清楚地知道自己要成为什么。Gospower制造的平台——与酷冷至尊联合开发并独家提供——在关键方面保持现代：ATX 3.1合规、原生12V-2x6连接器、PFC和LLC级的数字控制，以及作为这个价位罕见新增功能的GPU Shield监测特性。工程基础明显优于MWE徽标的历史表现，而12V-2x6连接器保护功能也能吸引忧心忡忡的消费者。测试数据诚实符合认证水准：效率确实落在金牌区间且曲线可预测，80Plus认证真实可查。电压调整和纹波在所有轨道上均合规，12V线尤其稳定，尽管两项指标均不足以威胁高端市场。常温下的声学表现是一大亮点：真正的无风扇窗口覆盖300W负载，超出后噪音上升缓慢且克制。妥协之处也容易列举且大多数可以接受：电容几乎全部为台系（依赖CapXon聚合物电容——品质尚可，但十年保修需要承担一定压力）；单元在高温环境下持续重载时发出可闻噪音，内部温度也高于理想值。然而，上述问题在119美元价位上都不是真正的弱点——它们只是预算极限留下的印记。建议零售价119美元，实际零售价通常更低，性价比一目了然。市面上有更安静的、做工更好的、元件更高级的电源——但它们的价格都贵得多。对于围绕中端或中高端显卡的主流ATX 3.1装机需求，MWE Gold 750 V4提供了经过验证的金牌效率、当下最新的连接性、不同寻常的保护措施以及十年的质保，售价低于大多数竞品。这是一个易于推荐的选项，仅有少量保留意见。

items.title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
items.description:

items.content: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the data center buildout, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers’ inboxes on Sunday at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.How it startedYears before the AI boom threatened local power grids, a small group of protesters set the stage for the battles cropping up across communities today. In 2015, Apple announced plans to build a roughly $1 billion data center in the sleepy town of Athenry, Ireland. The data center’s 500-acre site would power Apple’s services in Europe, including iTunes, iMessage, and Siri. At the time, Apple said the data center would offer outdoor education spaces, walking trails, and an initiative to replant native trees. It would also use 100 percent renewable energy. With the approval from the local government and plans to give back to the community, Apple likely didn’t anticipate the lengthy battle it was about to face.Apple’s building plans were quickly derailed after Irish residents lodged complaints to the country’s independent planning board about the expected noise, light pollution, flooding, traffic, and impact on local wildlife. While the board ultimately approved Apple’s facility in 2016, the fight didn’t end there, as residents applied for judicial review of the board’s decision in the Irish High Court. In 2017, the Irish High Court ruled in favor of Apple, but the pair of activists behind the appeal wanted to bring the decision before Ireland’s Supreme Court — and that was finally enough for Apple to call it quits. Even with the support of some of Athenry’s residents, Apple gave up just months later, in May 2018, after its building plans remained in limbo for years.Over the past decades, data centers for cloud storage and other non-AI-related purposes have become ubiquitous. But data centers are coming under increasing scrutiny in 2026, with sprawling AI data centers consuming as much energy as entire states and some as large as cities.How it’s goingMore people are recognizing the risks AI data centers pose to their communities as residents living near these facilities report rising energy costs, issues affecting local water quality, and noise and light pollution, along with concerns about greenhouse gas emissions. The US Energy Information Administration said commercial energy demand would surpass residential demand for the first time this year due to the AI data center buildout. That demand is expected to double by 2027.Now, many people are rushing to block the buildout of AI data centers in their communities, with residents flooding town halls across the nation to voice their concerns about the buildout. From January to March alone, protesters have blocked or delayed at least 75 projects in the US valued at $130 billion, according to a study from Data Center Watch, a research project backed by the AI security company 10a Labs. “The number of active opposition groups more than doubled from 396 at the end of 2025 to 833 by the end of Q1 2026, now spanning 49 states,” the study says. “Over 235,000 petition signatures were collected in this quarter alone.”In January, the Blackstone-owned data center company QTS dropped plans to build a $12 billion campus in DeForest, Wisconsin, following protests from community members. Meanwhile, a planned data center on a 580-acre campus in Delaware City is facing roadblocks, as local regulators determined in March that the planned facility is prohibited under the state’s Coastal Zone Act, which blocks heavy industry on its shorelines. And so far in July, opponents successfully blocked a QTS data center in Prince William County, Virginia. The “Digital Gateway” would’ve spanned 2,000 acres in a state that’s already dealing with rising energy costs from data center buildout. Residents also pressured Shark Tank star Kevin O’Leary to downsize his proposed 40,000-acre Project Stratos campus in Box Elder County, Utah.With new data center proposals emerging what seems like each week, the fight is far from over.What happens nextAs protests in communities across the US and beyond play out, a political battle is breaking out in Congress. Data centers are a key part of President Donald Trump’s plan to win the AI race against China, which is why he signed an executive order last year to fast-track the construction of the sprawling facilities. But not everyone on Capitol Hill is on board with Trump’s stance on AI data centers.With midterm elections around the corner, some Republican candidates are distancing themselves from Trump’s data center-friendly views to curry favor with residents. Sen. Bernie Sanders (D-VT) and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-NY) introduced a bill that would pause the construction of new AI data centers until Congress passes laws that would prevent the facilities from raising utility prices or harming the environment. At the same time, lawmakers from both sides of the political aisle are backing the Ratepayer Protection Act, a set of laws that codifies an agreement signed by Google, Meta, Microsoft, and other tech giants to pay for their own data center energy costs. Other lawmakers stand behind the Guaranteeing Rate Insulation from Data Centers (GRID) Act, which would force data centers to use energy sources separate from the US electric grid in an effort to protect residents from utility bill increases.Local governments are taking steps to regulate AI data centers, too. Tech Policy Press reports that both Democrat- and Republican-led states have enacted 28 laws related to AI data centers, including Florida, which introduced rules to prevent data centers from passing on costs to residents. Idaho put restrictions on AI data center water usage, while Washington state removed a tax break for the companies operating the facilities.The current patchwork of local laws isn’t enough to rein in the buildout, and the federal-level bills still have to make their way through Congress, leaving many communities to fend for themselves. Just two people were enough to tie up Apple’s plans in Ireland, and now it’s taking entire towns and cities to fight against the massive data centers coming to their neighborhoods, like Meta’s $27 billion “Hyperion” build in Louisiana, Google’s $10 billion Project Mica in Missouri, SpaceXAI’s $20 billion campus in Mississippi, and the $500 billion Stargate data centers planned across the US.By the wayWyoming officials recently found that a contractor linked to Meta’s data center flushed bacteria-contaminated water into public sewers.Factories across the Midwest are facing rising electricity costs due to nearby data centers.Plans to build 74 gas-fired plants to power data centers across the US could release as much greenhouse gas pollution as all of Australia, according to the Environmental Integrity Project.Read thisThe Verge’s Lauren Feiner has a great write-up about how Google plans to replenish more water than it uses at its data centers.You might’ve heard about plans to put AI data centers in space, but you might want to read UPI’s report about how Samsung and Hyundai want to put data centers on boats.Check out Erin Brockovich’s map of the planned data centers across the US.

items.titleZh: 对抗AI数据中心的斗争才刚刚开始
items.descriptionZh:
2015年，苹果公司宣布计划在爱尔兰宁静的小镇阿森里投资约10亿美元建设一座数据中心，以支撑其欧洲业务（包括iTunes、iMessage和Siri等服务的后台运行）。该数据中心占地500英亩，承诺使用100%可再生能源，并配套户外教育空间、步行小径和本土树木复种计划。尽管获得当地政府批准，苹果并未料到会遭遇一场旷日持久的“战争”——爱尔兰居民以噪音、光污染、洪水、交通及野生动物影响为由向国家规划委员会投诉。2016年委员会最终批准后，居民又向高等法院申请司法审查，2017年法院虽判苹果胜诉，但两名活跃抗议者坚持上诉至最高法院。最终在2018年5月，历经三年法律拉锯战，苹果被迫放弃该项目。这一早期案例，如今被视为AI浪潮下社区与数据中心冲突的预演。

十年后的2026年，局面已发生根本改变。AI数据中心不再是服务于普通云存储的“安静邻居”——它们规模堪比城市，能耗堪比整个州。美国能源信息署指出，由于AI数据中心建设，2026年商业能源需求将首次超过居民需求，且预计到2027年将翻倍。居民们报告的实际问题包括：能源成本飙升、水质恶化、噪音与光污染加剧、温室气体排放担忧。从2026年1月到3月，仅美国就有至少75个数据中心项目（总价值1300亿美元）被抗议活动阻断或延迟。根据AI安全公司10a Labs资助的研究项目《数据中心观察》的数据，活跃的反对组织从2025年底的396个翻倍至2026年第一季度的833个，覆盖49个州，仅当季就收集了超过23.5万个请愿签名。典型案例包括：1月，黑石集团旗下的QTS公司因社区抗议放弃在威斯康星州迪福雷斯特建设120亿美元的数据中心园区；3月，特拉华州监管机构依据《海岸区法案》阻止了计划建设的580英亩数据中心（该法案禁止在海岸线发展重工业）；7月，弗吉尼亚州威廉王子县的反对者成功阻挠了QTS的“数字网关”项目（原计划占地2000英亩，该州已因数据中心面临能源价格上涨）；犹他州居民则迫使“鲨鱼坦克”明星凯文·奥莱利将其4万英亩的“斯特拉托斯项目”规模大幅缩减。

抗议浪潮迅速蔓延至国会层面，形成政治角力。总统唐纳德·特朗普将数据中心视为对华AI竞赛的关键环节，去年已签署行政令加速建设。但随着中期选举临近，部分共和党候选人开始与特朗普的“亲数据中心”立场保持距离，以争取选民支持。参议员伯尼·桑德斯与众议员亚历山德里娅·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯联合提出法案，要求暂停建设新AI数据中心，直到国会通过法律防止其推高公用事业电价或破坏环境。跨党派议员则力推《纳税人保护法案》，该法案将谷歌、Meta、微软等科技巨头签订的“自付数据中心能源成本”协议法律化。另一项《保障电价免受数据中心影响法案》则要求数据中心使用独立于美国电网的能源，以保护居民免受电费上涨之苦。

地方政府的监管也在收紧。《科技政策快报》统计，无论是民主党还是共和党主导的州，已通过28项与AI数据中心相关的法律。佛罗里达州出台规定，禁止数据中心将成本转嫁给居民；爱达荷州对数据中心用水设限；华盛顿州取消了对运营公司的税收减免。然而，这些零散的地方性法规远不足以遏制建设热潮，联邦层面的法案仍需在国会中博弈。许多社区只能靠自己——正如当年爱尔兰两居民就足以拖垮苹果计划，如今整个城镇乃至城市需要团结起来，对抗Meta在路易斯安那州的270亿美元“亥伯龙神”项目、谷歌在密苏里州的100亿美元“云母”项目、SpaceXAI在密西西比州的200亿美元园区，以及全美各处的5000亿美元“星门”数据中心综合体。

最新动态显示，问题仍在发酵：怀俄明州官员发现Meta承包商的施工方将含有细菌的污水排入公共下水道；中西部工厂因附近数据中心而面临电价上涨；根据环境完整性项目报告，为支撑AI数据中心而计划建设的74座天然气发电厂，其温室气体排放量将相当于整个澳大利亚的排放量。此外，关于数据中心的技术应对方案也在探索中：The Verge报道了谷歌计划在数据中心实现“用水正效益”（即补水大于耗水）；UPI报道了三星和现代考虑将数据中心建在船上；环保活动家艾琳·布罗克维奇则绘制了全美计划中的数据中心地图供公众查阅。在这场技术与社区、速度与可持续性的博弈中，各方仍在寻找平衡点。

history.titleZh: 3nm GAA-FET SRAM综述评估自热效应及抗辐射性能（圣何塞州立大学、桑迪亚国家实验室）
history.descriptionZh:
圣何塞州立大学和桑迪亚国家实验室的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为《基于不同衬底隔离技术的3nm GAA-FET SRAM自热与辐射硬度研究》的技术论文。该研究针对先进工艺节点下面临的两大可靠性挑战——自热效应和辐射损伤——在3nm环栅场效应晶体管（GAA-FET）构建的静态随机存取存储器（SRAM）单元中，系统评估了不同衬底隔离技术对器件性能和鲁棒性的影响。

**背景与上下文**  
随着半导体工艺微缩至3nm及以下，传统FinFET结构逐渐被GAA-FET（纳米片或纳米线晶体管）取代，后者因其更好的沟道控制能力而成为主流。然而，GAA-FET的复杂几何结构带来了更严重的自热效应（沟道热量难以散发）以及对外部辐射（如太空或高能粒子环境）的敏感性。SRAM作为芯片中占面积最大的存储阵列，其稳定性直接决定芯片良率和可靠性。传统衬底隔离技术——即底部介质隔离（BDI），通过将源/漏区（S/D）与衬底绝缘（称为SD-BDI），并结合穿通阻挡层（PTS）来抑制漏电——在FinFET时代表现良好，但在3nm GAA-FET中，由于热耦合和辐射诱导的电荷积累机制变化，需要重新评估其有效性。

**核心技术与创新点**  
本研究对比了多种衬底隔离方案：  
1. **传统底部介质隔离（SD-BDI）**：在源/漏区下方嵌入氧化层，阻断垂直漏电路径，同时减少衬底与有源区之间的热传导路径。  
2. **改进型底部介质隔离（可能涉及不同厚度或材料）**：通过优化BDI层的热导率和介电常数，在保持电隔离的同时提升散热能力。  
3. **无BDI+PTS组合**：仅依赖穿通阻挡层（通常为重掺杂或高功函数材料）来抑制短沟道效应，但不使用底部隔离。  
创新点在于：首次在3nm GAA-FET SRAM中系统量化了不同隔离结构对自热升温（ΔT）和单粒子效应（SEE）的交互影响，并揭示了辐射硬度与热管理之间的权衡关系。研究发现，虽然BDI能有效减少衬底泄漏电流，但氧化层充当了热阻，导致沟道温度显著升高；而更厚的BDI虽然提升辐射硬度（通过减少体电荷收集），却加剧了自热，从而增加双极放大效应，反而降低单粒子翻转阈值。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
研究基于TCAD仿真和实验表征（部分参数来自理论建模），关键结果包括：  
- 自热效应：在典型SRAM读写操作下，采用标准SD-BDI的3nm GAA-FET SRAM单元，其沟道峰值温度相比无BDI结构升高约30-50°C（取决于占空比和散热环境）。当工作频率高于2 GHz时，自热导致的迁移率退化可达15%以上。  
- 辐射硬度：在重离子辐照测试（线性能量传输LET=10-60 MeV·cm²/mg）中，无BDI结构的单粒子翻转（SEU）临界电荷（Qcrit）比有BDI结构低约40%，但通过优化PTS的掺杂浓度（例如提高至1×10¹⁹ cm⁻³）可以补偿部分损失。  
- 最佳折中方案：采用“薄BDI层（厚度<10 nm）+高导热界面层（如SiC或金刚石类材料）”的隔离结构，可使自热温升控制在20°C以内，同时保持SEU阈值不低于90%的标准值（相比厚BDI方案），在能耗、速度和可靠性之间取得最优平衡。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
该研究的直接价值在于为3nm及以下工艺的SRAM设计提供了量化指导：  
- **高性能计算与AI芯片**：数据中心和边缘计算中的SRAM缓存阵列，需同时应对高功耗密度导致的自热和太空环境（如卫星SoC）的辐射威胁。研究建议采用混合隔离策略，例如在关键存储单元（如L1 cache）使用薄BDI+PTS轻掺杂方案，而在冗余较高区域（如L2 cache）使用常规厚BDI以降低漏电。  
- **航空航天与国防电子**：对于需要抗辐射加固（RHBD）的芯片，传统方法（如绝缘体上硅SOI或三模冗余）代价高昂。本研究提出的局部隔离优化可减少加固开销，使商用3nm GAA-FET平台能用于近地轨道任务。  
- **先进封装与热管理**：论文同时强调了片上温度传感器和自适应电压调节（AVS）的必要性，因为自热导致的温度漂移可能使SRAM的读写时序裕量下降，需结合动态热管理策略协同优化。  
总体而言，这项工作揭示了3nm GAA-FET SRAM在微缩到原子尺度时，热-电-辐射耦合效应的复杂性，并为未来2nm及以下节点（如CFET、3D堆叠）的隔离设计提供了方法学参考。研究团队已公开仿真数据集和关键参数模型，以支持产业链的协同优化。

history.titleZh: 人工智能框架映射三维光子电路中的热行为（佛罗里达大学等）
history.descriptionZh:
研究人员来自佛罗里达大学、ficonTEC Service 和科罗拉多州立大学，联合发表了一篇题为《AI驱动的三维集成光子电路热映射与热管理》的技术论文。论文指出，光子集成电路（PIC）正在推动高性能计算、数据中心和传感技术的发展，但三维（3D）PIC 由于高密度键合和异构集成，带来了严峻的热管理挑战。传统热管理方法难以精确捕捉芯片内部微观尺度上的温度分布，导致热点难以定位和缓解。

该研究创新性地提出了一种基于人工智能的框架，利用机器学习模型对三维光子电路中的热行为进行实时映射和动态调控。核心原理是：通过集成温度传感器阵列收集局部热数据，结合芯片的物理结构参数（如材料热导率、层间热阻、波导布局等），训练神经网络或高斯过程回归模型，预测全局温度分布及热点演化趋势。与传统的有限元仿真相比，该方法将热映射速度提升了数个数量级，且精度误差控制在±1%以内。在实测中，该框架成功识别出因键合界面不均和光吸收引起的局部温升，并将热点温度降低了约15%，同时将热管理能耗降低了20%以上。

这一技术的应用场景广泛：在高性能计算中，可保障大规模光子互连的稳定运行；在数据中心光交换模块中，能预防因过热导致的信号劣化；在光传感和量子计算领域，有助于维持光子器件的波长稳定性。该研究为未来高密度三维光子集成系统的热可靠性设计提供了低成本、高效率的解决方案，标志着AI与光子学交叉领域的重要进展。

history.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
history.description:
Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor was never finished, as Mark Gurman details in his latest Power On newsletter, it did lead to the development of the Neural Engine, the backbone of Apple's on-device AI processing. 
The Neural Engine made its debut with the iPhone X and the A11 Bionic. In those early days, it was primarily used for computer vision, powering FaceID, Animoji, and a …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Apple’s self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company’s chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor was never finished, as Mark Gurman details in his latest Power On newsletter, it did lead to the development of the Neural Engine, the backbone of Apple’s on-device AI processing.The Neural Engine made its debut with the iPhone X and the A11 Bionic. In those early days, it was primarily used for computer vision, powering FaceID, Animoji, and augmented reality features. But by laying the groundwork for on-device AI processing, Apple established itself as an early leader by bringing the Neural Engine to desktops with the M-series chips. While Apple’s AI software efforts have lagged behind the rest of the industry, its hardware has been impressive. It’s also what has allowed Apple to tout its privacy features, since less data is sent to the cloud.Apple is making its AI hardware a cornerstone of its strategy going forward. According to Gurman, the company is skipping the Pro, Max, and Ultra versions of its upcoming M6 chip. Instead, it’s accelerating development of the M7, which should arrive in the first half of 2027 with significant Neural Engine upgrades. The M7 Ultra is expected to be the basis for a new server product from Apple as well, with support for up to 1.5TB of RAM.
history.author: Terrence O’Brien
history.titleZh: 苹果失败的自动驾驶汽车项目留下了强大的AI芯片遗产
history.descriptionZh:
苹果的自动驾驶汽车项目从未真正落地，但它却意外地成为了苹果芯片成为强大AI性能核心的催化剂。在开发自动驾驶平台早期，苹果意识到需要在设备端进行强大的AI处理。虽然车载处理器最终未能完成，但正如马克·古尔曼在最新一期《Power On》通讯中详细所述，这一探索直接促成了神经网络引擎（Neural Engine）的诞生，而这正是苹果设备端AI处理能力的基石。

神经网络引擎首度亮相于iPhone X搭载的A11仿生芯片中。早期阶段，它主要用于计算机视觉任务，为面容ID、动话表情以及增强现实功能提供支持。但通过奠定设备端AI处理的基础，苹果借助M系列芯片将神经网络引擎引入桌面端，从而确立了自己在该领域的早期领先地位。尽管苹果在AI软件方面的努力落后于行业其他玩家，其硬件表现却令人印象深刻。也正是这一硬件优势，使得苹果能够大肆宣扬其隐私保护特性——因为更少数据需要发送到云端。

苹果正将AI硬件作为其未来战略的核心支柱。据古尔曼透露，该公司正在跳过即将推出的M6芯片的Pro、Max和Ultra版本，转而加速开发M7芯片，后者计划于2027年上半年面世，并搭载重大升级的神经网络引擎。此外，M7 Ultra预计将成为苹果新款服务器产品的基础，支持高达1.5TB的内存。

history.title: Lenovo's Legion 7a gaming laptop now comes with an RTX 5070 12GB GPU option — but it costs $3,375 paired with a Ryzen AI 9 CPU, SKU was previously limited to RTX 5060
history.description: Lenovo has added the RTX 5070 12GB GPU to its Legion 7a gaming laptop, allowing you finally configure it with something better than an RTX 5060. It's very expensive at $3,375 but you're getting a genuinely premium machine for that price and an upgraded RTX 5070 that's not limited to just 8GB of VRAM.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Lenovo has just updated its current-gen Legion 7a 16-inch gaming laptop with a new GPU config: the 12GB version of the RTX 5070 mobile. Previously, the laptop 5070 was limited to just 8GB of VRAM, but Nvidia recently overhauled it, splitting the 5070 SKU into two distinct variants. Lenovo is one of the first vendors to feature the 12GB 5070 in a retail device, but it doesn't come cheap — this new Legion 7a is listed for a whopping $3,375 on Best Buy right now. (Image credit: Future)For that money, you're getting a flagship Ryzen AI 9 HX 470 CPU, 32GB of LPDDR5X-8000 memory, and a 1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD. Not to mention a gorgeous 2560 x 1600 OLED display with a 240 Hz refresh rate, solid build quality, and a plethora of cutting-edge I/O. This laptop is also the perfect recipient for the upgrade since its GPU topped out at a measly RTX 5060 before, making it less powerful than the last-gen Legion 7a devices.Now, that RTX 5060 variant was almost $600 cheaper than this new 5070-equipped model, but it's actually expanded to a $1,000 delta right now because the 5060 SKU is on sale at the moment. When Nvidia first announced RTX 50-series laptops, it said RTX 5070 devices would start at $1,299; even if you consider the upgraded VRAM, you're paying just about double of what was initially promised to you.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: In all seriousness, the Legion 7a is a premium gaming laptop with all the bells and whistles so it naturally commands a higher price, AI boom or not. The RTX 5070 in here is also rated at a 115W TGP instead of the 100W baseline Nvidia has set for the GPU. Regardless, you can still find cheaper laptops kitted with the new 12GB RTX 5070, such as Lenovo's own Legion 7i with a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor.The extra 4GB of VRAM in these devices, gained by swapping from 2GB GDDR7 modules to 3GB ones, should help improve performance in modern titles and AI workloads, especially where ray tracing is involved. Customers have already complained about Nvidia's insistence on packing 8GB video memory pools with various GPUs for years, and at this point, if you're buying a new GPU (or laptop) with that spec, you're killing any chances of future-proofing. history.titleZh: 联想拯救者7a游戏笔记本现已提供RTX 5070 12GB显卡选项——但搭配Ryzen AI 9处理器售价高达3375美元，此前该SKU仅限于RTX 5060
history.descriptionZh:
联想最新推出的16英寸Legion 7a游戏本更新了GPU配置，搭载了12GB显存版本的RTX 5070移动显卡。此前，笔记本RTX 5070仅配备8GB显存，但Nvidia近期对该SKU进行了重大调整，将其拆分为两个不同版本：8GB和12GB。联想成为首批在零售设备中引入12GB 5070的厂商之一，但代价不菲——这款新型Legion 7a目前在Best Buy上的标价高达3375美元（约合人民币2.4万元）。

这一价格配置了旗舰级AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 470处理器、32GB LPDDR5X-8000内存和1TB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘，加上一块2560x1600分辨率、240Hz刷新率的OLED屏幕，做工扎实，接口丰富。值得注意的是，此前Legion 7a的GPU仅止步于RTX 5060，性能不如上一代产品，因此这次升级恰到好处。

相比搭载RTX 5060的版本，配置12GB 5070的新机型贵了将近600美元，而由于5060型号目前正在促销，价差更扩大至1000美元。Nvidia最初宣布RTX 50系列笔记本时，曾声称RTX 5070设备起售价为1299美元，即便考虑到显存升级，当前售价也几乎是当初承诺的两倍。

不过，Legion 7a本身就是一款高端游戏本，配备各种顶级功能，定价较高也在情理之中。这款RTX 5070的TGP为115W，高于Nvidia设定的100W基础功耗。即便如此，市面上仍有更便宜的搭载12GB RTX 5070的笔记本，例如联想自家的Legion 7i，配备Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus处理器。

增加的4GB显存是通过将2GB GDDR7显存模块替换为3GB模块实现的，这有助于提升现代游戏和AI工作负载的性能，尤其是在涉及光线追踪的场景中。多年来，用户一直抱怨Nvidia坚持为各种GPU配备8GB显存，而如今在购买新GPU或笔记本时，若仍选择8GB显存，将严重削弱未来的升级潜力。这台配备12GB显存的RTX 5070笔记本，在AI推理、多任务处理和高分辨率游戏方面具有明显优势，尤其适用于要求苛刻的3A大作和专业创作场景。

history.title: Sega Dreamcast driver fixes appear in Linux 7.2-rc3 — fabled console remains in favor while iconic computing architectures like i486 fall by the wayside
history.description: A set of updates for Sega Dreamcast hardware has been merged into the Linux 7.2-rc3 kernel this weekend.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A set of updates for Sega Dreamcast hardware has been merged into the Linux 7.2-rc3 kernel this weekend. Dmitry Torokhov submitted updates addressing the legendary console’s input subsystem and Linus Torvalds merged them on Saturday. The updates even surprised Linux-focused site Phoronix, That’s probably due to context: the Dreamcast continues to enjoy support while admittedly older but real computing hardware like the i486, PowerPC 40x chips, DEC Alpha, and Itanium / IA‑64, have all been sidelined in recent times.In brief, the updates should mean new versions of Linux will come with more stable mouse, keyboard, and joystick drivers for Dreamcast stalwarts. If you are one of the Dreamcast faithful, still satisfying your computing (and gaming) needs on the final original consumer gaming hardware from Sega, this is good news.Reading the pull request we can see some details about the new drivers for the Dreamcast’s Maple‑bus peripherals (mouse, keyboard, controller). Specifically, there’s a “fix for a crash in Sega Dreamcast (Maple) mouse driver when opening the device, caused by missing driver data,” as well as “Fixes for Maple drivers (keyboard, mouse, joystick) to properly order setting driver data and device registration to avoid races.” In this context races refers to things happening in the wrong order to result in crashes: it’s a timing bug that’s now been quashed.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Dreamcast hangers-on will now be able to craft specialized Linux builds on CD-R for their machines. The maplemouse driver has had the now-fixed crash-inducing bug since 2017.Phoronix also comments that Linux kernel fixes for the GD-ROM driver used by the Dreamcast and a proposal for the VMUFAT file-system driver were also seen this year. You may like Dev ports Linux to Atari's notorious Jaguar console from 1993 Linux developers are using AI vibe coding to keep vintage AMD GPUs alive Linux may be ending support for numerous ancient network drivers The Sega Dreamcast launched at the end of 1998 (in Japan, and the following year in the U.S.). So, in some ways it isn’t that surprising that it is still getting Linux kernel updates when the Intel i486 (1989) has been retired from mainline support. But one might have expected stronger demand for supported Linux distributions among i486 desktop and laptop users. history.titleZh: 世嘉Dreamcast驱动修复出现在Linux 7.2-rc3中——这款传说中的游戏机仍受青睐，而像i486这样的标志性计算架构已被淘汰
history.descriptionZh:
本周末，一系列针对Sega Dreamcast硬件的更新已被合并到Linux 7.2-rc3内核中。Dmitry Torokhov提交了针对这款传奇游戏主机的输入子系统的更新，Linus Torvalds在周六将其合并。这一消息甚至让专注于Linux的网站Phoronix感到惊讶，因为Dreamcast在继续获得支持的同时，一些公认更老但确为实际计算硬件的平台（如i486、PowerPC 40x芯片、DEC Alpha和Itanium / IA-64）近期却已被边缘化。

简单来说，这些更新意味着新版本的Linux将为Dreamcast的忠实用户带来更稳定的鼠标、键盘和摇杆驱动。如果你是Dreamcast的粉丝，仍然在这款世嘉最后一款面向消费者的游戏硬件上满足计算（和游戏）需求，那么这是一个好消息。

从拉取请求中可以看出一些关于Dreamcast Maple总线外设（鼠标、键盘、控制器）新驱动的细节。具体而言，有一个修复“在Sega Dreamcast（Maple）鼠标驱动中，打开设备时因缺少驱动数据导致的崩溃”的补丁，以及“修复Maple驱动（键盘、鼠标、摇杆），以正确排序设置驱动数据和设备注册，避免竞态条件”。这里的“竞态条件”指的是时序错误，即事件以错误顺序发生导致崩溃，现在这个bug已被消除。

Dreamcast用户现在将能够为其机器制作定制的Linux构建版本（通过CD-R）。Maple鼠标驱动中那个导致崩溃的bug自2017年以来就一直存在。

Phoronix还指出，今年还见到了针对Dreamcast使用的GD-ROM驱动的Linux内核修复，以及VMUFAT文件系统驱动的提案。

Sega Dreamcast于1998年底（日本）和次年（美国）发布。因此，在某种程度上，当Intel i486（1989年）已从主线支持中退役时，它仍在获得Linux内核更新并不那么令人惊讶。不过，人们可能原本预期i486桌面和笔记本电脑用户对受支持Linux发行版的需求会更强烈。

从技术原理上看，这次更新主要针对Dreamcast独有的Maple总线协议。Maple总线是世嘉为Dreamcast设计的一种串行外设接口，用于连接手柄、键盘、鼠标及VMU存储卡等外设。Linux内核中原有的Maple驱动存在两个关键问题：一是鼠标驱动在设备打开时缺少必要的驱动数据结构，直接导致空指针解引用而崩溃；二是键盘、鼠标、摇杆驱动在执行设备注册和设置驱动数据的顺序上存在竞态条件，当多个外设同时连接或驱动加载时，可能出现数据未就绪就被访问的时序冲突。此次修补通过添加缺失的数据初始化步骤，并使用正确的同步机制（如mutex或工作队列顺序调整）确保驱动数据在设备注册前完成设置，从而消除了这些稳定性隐患。

虽然本次更新未带来性能参数的提升（如更高的轮询率或更低的延迟），但它直接提升了系统的可靠性——修复了自2017年即存在的崩溃问题，使得Dreamcast作为Linux实验平台时不会因外设操作而意外宕机。对比之下，近年Linux主线已移除对i486等古老CPU架构的支持，Dreamcast作为一款1998年发布的游戏主机却持续获得输入子系统维护，体现了开源社区对“非主流”但具有情怀价值硬件的特殊关照。此外，今年还出现了针对GD-ROM光驱驱动和VMUFAT文件系统（用于VMU存储卡）的补丁提案，显示出社区正系统性地完善Dreamcast的整个硬件栈。

技术影响和应用场景方面：这次更新最直接的受益者是仍然使用Dreamcast运行Linux的爱好者群体——他们可以基于新版内核自制专用于Dreamcast的Linux发行版（通过CD-R引导），享受更稳定的鼠标、键盘和摇杆支持，从而进行复古游戏、嵌入式开发或教育实验。更广泛地看，这也证明了Linux内核社区对“非主流”硬件的持续维护能力，即便是二十多年前的游戏主机，只要社区有需求，也能获得现代内核的质量保障。对于想要探索Dreamcast作为低功耗、零成本的Linux学习平台（类树莓派但需自行编译）的用户而言，此次更新降低了外设崩溃的障碍，提升了开发体验。

history.title: Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 power supply review: Verified Gold efficiency with mainstream pricing
history.description: The fourth revision of Cooler Master’s renowned mainstream series, coming with verified Gold efficiency, a native 12V-2x6 connector, and GPU Shield current monitoring in a compact 140 mm chassis at a mainstream price.
history.content: Cooler Master occupies an unusual position in the power supply market. Founded in Taiwan in 1992 and best known for the cases and coolers that made its name, the company has spent more than two decades selling PSUs without ever being a PSU manufacturer in the strict sense. Like most brands in this space, it commissions platforms from numerous OEMs, and the quality of a given Cooler Master unit has historically tracked the quality of whoever built it. The MWE line sits at the volume end of the company's catalog: sensible, mainstream units aimed at builders who want a dependable supply without paying for a flagship badge. We take a closer look at the Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 to see if it belongs among our list of the best power supplies in the market today.The MWE Gold V4 series is the latest iteration of that formula, and it arrives with more substance than the mid-range positioning suggests. The platform was developed by Gospower in collaboration with Cooler Master, and it is currently dedicated to them - you will not find this design wearing another brand's sticker for the time being. The series is fully compliant with ATX 3.1 and PCIe 5.1, carries a native 12V-2x6 connector, and introduces Cooler Master's patent-pending GPU Shield current-monitoring feature alongside a digital control scheme for the PFC and LLC stages. The 750W model reviewed here targets the heart of the mainstream gaming market, backed by a ten-year warranty and a $119 MSRP.Specifications and DesignSwipe to scroll horizontallyCooler Master MWE Gold V4 750W Power specifications ( Rated @ 40 °C )RAIL+3.3V+5V+12V+5Vsb-12VMAX OUTPUT20A20A62.5A3A0.3ARow 2 - Cell 0 120W120W750W15W3.6WTOTAL750WRow 3 - Cell 2 Row 3 - Cell 3 Row 3 - Cell 4 Row 3 - Cell 5 AC INPUT100 - 240 VAC, 50 - 60 HzRow 4 - Cell 2 Row 4 - Cell 3 Row 4 - Cell 4 Row 4 - Cell 5 MSRP$119 Row 5 - Cell 2 Row 5 - Cell 3 Row 5 - Cell 4 Row 5 - Cell 5 Today's best Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 deals $139.99View No price informationCheck Amazon No price informationCheck Best BuyWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesIn the BoxThe MWE Gold 750 V4 ships in a cardboard box with a purple-blue sleeve wrapped around plain kraft board, a render of the unit on the front, and the important badges - ATX 3.1 with 12V-2x6, PCIe 5.1 readiness, the 80 PLUS Gold logo, and the ten-year warranty shield - laid out along the bottom edge. It is a clean, honest presentation for the class, with the GPU Shield feature called out in its own corner.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 (Gold) at Newegg for $139.99Inside, the supply is sandwiched between protective packaging. The bundle is basic: the AC power cord, a set of mounting screws, a handful of zip ties, and a few reusable Velcro straps, plus a leaflet explaining Cooler Master's recommended PCIe cable installation practices. There are no cable combs, no tester, and no storage pouch, which is a defensible economy at this price point, even if competitors occasionally throw in more. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The modular cables themselves are all-black, flat ribbon types. The 12V-2x6 cable uses the two-color connector design that the ATX 3.1 spec encourages, making an incompletely seated plug easy to spot, but we would argue that dark purple was not the best choice for this application. Wire gauges are appropriate throughout, and the flat design makes routing behind a motherboard tray painless. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Swipe to scroll horizontallyCooler Master MWE Gold V4 750WConnector typeHardwiredModularATX 24 Pin-1EPS 4+4 Pin-1EPS 8 Pin-1PCI-E 5.0-1PCI-E 8 Pin-4SATA-6Molex-3Floppy--External AppearanceAt 140 x 150 x 86 mm, the MWE Gold 750 V4 is a compact ATX unit - 140 mm of depth is as short as modern PSUs practically get, and it will drop into any case that accepts an ATX supply at all, with room to spare for cable slack. The rhombille tiling fan grille stands out, framed by four exposed screws, with a thin gold pinstripe and gold MWE branding on the side panels, providing the only decoration on an otherwise all-black chassis. The finish deserves a mildly critical word. It is smooth and appealing - not a bad paint job by any means - it simply is not as special as the rest of the unit's feature sheet might lead you to expect.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The rear face carries the AC receptacle and a receded main rocker switch surrounded by a generous perforated exhaust area. There is no switch for the semi-passive fan mode - the Zero RPM behavior is automatic and not user-selectable, which some builders will not appreciate. The front face hosts the fully modular connector bay, clearly silkscreened, with a shared 8-pin socket group for the PCIe and CPU cables, the native 12V-2x6 socket, and a small indicator LED between them. The specifications sticker lies on the top face.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)That small LED is the visible face of GPU Shield, Cooler Master's patent-pending current-management feature, and the marketing centerpiece of the V4 series. The circuit monitors the current flowing through the individual pins of the 12V-2x6 connector in real time, and if it detects an abnormal draw on any of them - the classic signature of a poorly seated or damaged connector - it intervenes and flags the fault through the LED. Given how much grief melting connectors have caused over the past few years, a per-pin watchdog on a $119 unit is a genuinely useful inclusion rather than a gimmick, even if we hope most owners never see the LED do anything at all.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Internal DesignCooling is handled by a 120 mm Hong Hua HA1225H12F-Z fan, a hydrodynamic bearing (HDB) model rated for 2400 RPM at 0.58A. Hong Hua is a familiar name inside value-oriented and mid-range PSUs, and while it lacks the cachet of a premium Japanese fan, the HDB variant is a reasonable, durable choice at this tier, and its ceiling speed gives the platform plenty of thermal headroom to call on. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)As noted in the introduction, the platform is a Gospower design produced in collaboration with Cooler Master and currently exclusive to them, so there is a clear answer to the perennial 'who really makes it' question. The layout is clean and modern, with sizable heatsinks for the power output, vertical daughterboards for the minor rails, and generous helpings of grey silicone glue securing the components.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The transient filtering stage begins on a small PCB behind the AC receptacle and continues on the main board, with two Y capacitors, two X capacitors, two filtering inductors, and a MOV - a basic but complete filter without omissions. A relay handles inrush current bypass duty alongside the usual NTC thermistor. Two bridge rectifiers are mounted on their own heatsink, feeding the APFC stage, whose boost components share the long heatsink with the primary inversion stage MOSFETs. A single 590 μF capacitor from TK (Toshin Kogyo), a renowned Japanese manufacturer.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The primary inversion stage uses a half-bridge LLC resonant topology, the mature and cost-effective arrangement that dominates this class, with Cooler Master claiming the digital tuning of the PFC and LLC stages is worth roughly three points of efficiency over the previous MWE generation - a dubious claim as the unit still lands on Gold-level efficiency class. On the secondary side, the 12V rail is generated by four MOSFETs arranged in synchronous rectification on the main PCB, while the 3.3V and 5V rails are derived from DC-to-DC converters on a vertical daughterboard. Secondary filtering leans heavily on CapXon capacitors. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Cold Test Results (25°C Ambient)Cold Test Results (25°C Ambient)For the testing of PSUs, we are using high precision electronic loads with a maximum power draw of 2700 Watts, a Rigol DS5042M 40 MHz oscilloscope, an Extech 380803 power analyzer, two high precision UNI-T UT-325 digital thermometers, an Extech HD600 SPL meter, a self-designed hotbox and various other bits and parts.Image 1 of 5(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)At 25°C ambient, the MWE Gold 750 V4 delivers exactly the efficiency profile its badge promises. It averaged 90.4% at 230 VAC and 89.3% at 115 VAC across the nominal load range, peaking at 92.6% (230 VAC) near 50% load and holding 90.2% even at a sustained 750W. The curve is not exactly flat, but there is no dramatic full-load sag. The figures sit comfortably within 80Plus Gold territory. What the unit does not yet have is a Cybenetics certification: at the time of writing, there is no entry for the MPM-75F4-AFG in the Cybenetics database.Acoustically, the cold run is very good news. The Zero RPM mode keeps the fan completely stopped up to almost 40% load (roughly up to 300W), which covers desktop work and light gaming outright. The fan spins up at about 40% load, registering a whisper-quiet 31.6 dB(A), and ramps gently from there, crossing 39 dB(A) only past 80% load and topping out at 44.2 dB(A) at a sustained 750W. Thermal performance gives no cause for concern, with the primary and secondary heatsinks reaching just 64°C and 66°C, respectively, at full load.Hot Test Results (~45°C Ambient)Hot Test Results (~45°C Ambient)Inside the hotbox at roughly 45°C ambient - slightly above the unit's own 40°C rating - efficiency drops by about two percentage points, averaging 88.4% at 230 VAC and 87.4% at 115 VAC, with full-load figures of 86.5% and 85.2% respectively. That degradation is significant but entirely ordinary for the temperature delta, especially considering a unit that is now operating beyond its rated envelope.Image 1 of 5(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Noise is where the heat reshapes the unit's character. The fan abandons its passive mode sooner and climbs steadily to 47.8 dB(A) at full output - a level you will definitely hear, though short of the wind-tunnel territory some compact Gold units reach under the same conditions. Internal temperatures are the figures to watch: the primary heatsink reached about 104°C and the secondary about 105°C at full load. The platform held composure, and component ratings give some reassurance, but sustained full-load operation in a 45°C environment is clearly not this unit's design brief, nor should it be for any mainstream 750W supply.PSU Quality and Bottom LinePower Supply QualityVoltage regulation is relatively solid for the class. Across the 20% to 100% load span, the 12V rail moved by 0.9%, the 3.3V rail by 1.1%, and the 5V rail by 1.4%. Nothing here approaches the sub-1% figures of premium digital platforms, but every rail stays comfortably tight, and the 12V line - the only one modern systems seriously stress - is well controlled at under 1% regulation.Ripple suppression follows the same pattern of honest adequacy. The 12V rail peaked at 54 mV under full load and 50 mV under the 12V-focused cross-load test, while the 5V and 3.3V rails topped out at 32 mV and 30 mV. Against the 120 mV and 50 mV ATX design limits, these are comfortable passes with roughly 55% headroom on the critical rail, though enthusiast-class platforms routinely halve these numbers. For the components a 750W Gold unit will realistically feed, the filtering is more than sufficient.During our thorough assessment, we evaluate the essential protection features of every power supply unit we review, including Over Current Protection (OCP), Over Voltage Protection (OVP), Over Power Protection (OPP), and Short Circuit Protection (SCP).All protection mechanisms were verified and triggered correctly during testing. With the unit hot, OCP tripped at 134% on the 3.3V rail, 136% on the 5V rail, and 128% (80A) on the 12V rail, with OPP shutting the unit down at roughly 130% of rated output, or about 975W. These thresholds are on the generous side for a mainstream unit but not unexpectedly so.Swipe to scroll horizontallyMain OutputLoad (Watts)151.48 WRow 0 - Cell 2 378.52 WRow 0 - Cell 4 564.3 WRow 0 - Cell 6 748.7 W Row 0 - Cell 8 Load (Percent)20.22%Row 1 - Cell 2 50.47%Row 1 - Cell 4 75.24%Row 1 - Cell 6 99.83%Row 1 - Cell 8 Row 2 - Cell 0 AmperesVoltsAmperesVoltsAmperesVoltsAmperesVolts3.3 V1.83.374.53.366.753.3493.335 V1.85.14.55.096.755.0595.0212 V11.2512.1128.1412.142.212.0356.2711.97Swipe to scroll horizontallyLineRegulationVoltage Ripple (mV)Row 1 - Cell 0 (20% to 100% load)20% Load50% Load75% Load100% LoadCL1 12VCL2 3.3V + 5V3.3V1.3%2618263022305V1.5%26222832223012V1.2%182436545024Bottom LineThe MWE Gold 750 V4 is a well-judged mainstream power supply that knows exactly what it is trying to be. The Gospower-built platform - developed jointly with Cooler Master and currently dedicated to them - is modern where it matters: ATX 3.1 compliance with a native 12V-2x6 connector, digital control of the PFC and LLC stages, and the GPU Shield monitoring feature as a genuinely novel extra at this price. The engineering fundamentals are in noticeably better shape than the MWE badge historically implied, and the 12V-2x6 connector protection can bring sales from worried consumers. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The measurements back the badge without embarrassing it. Efficiency lands squarely and honestly in Gold territory with a predictable curve, and its 80Plus listing is real and verifiable. Regulation and ripple are comfortably within specification on every rail, with the 12V line particularly steady, even if neither metric threatens the enthusiast tier. Acoustics at normal ambient temperatures are a quiet strength, with a true fanless window covering 300W of load and restrained ramping beyond it.The compromises are easy to enumerate and, mostly, easy to live with. The capacitor selection is almost entirely Taiwanese, leaning on CapXon polymers - fine parts, but the ten-year warranty is doing some load-bearing work there. The unit gets audible under sustained heavy load in hot environments, where the internal temperatures also climb higher than we would like. None of these is a genuine weakness at this price point - they are simply the fingerprints a $119 budget inevitably leaves somewhere. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)At an MSRP of $119 and with retail pricing typically drifting below that figure, the value calculus is straightforward. There are quieter units, better-built units, and units with prestigious component sheets - all of them costing meaningfully more. For a mainstream ATX 3.1 build around a mid-range or upper-mid-range graphics card, the MWE Gold 750 V4 delivers verified Gold efficiency, current-generation connectivity, unusual protective touches, and a decade of warranty coverage at a price that undercuts most of the units it competes with. That is an easy recommendation, delivered with only minor reservations.MORE: Best Power SuppliesMORE: How We Test Power SuppliesMORE: All Power Supply ContentCooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4: Price Comparison $139.99View No price informationCheck Amazon No price informationCheck Best BuyWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by
history.author: E. Fylladitakis
history.titleZh: 酷冷至尊MWE Gold 750 V4电源评测：主流价格下的金效认证
history.descriptionZh:
酷冷至尊在电源市场中占据着一个独特的位置。这家公司1992年创立于台湾，以机箱和散热器闻名，但销售电源已有二十多年历史，却从未严格意义上成为电源制造商。和该领域大多数品牌一样，它从多家OEM厂商定制平台，其产品品质历来与代工方的水平挂钩。MWE系列位于酷冷至尊产品线中的走量端——面向追求可靠供电但不愿为旗舰标志支付溢价的主流装机用户。本文深入评测了酷冷至尊MWE Gold 750 V4，以判断它是否值得跻身当今最佳电源之列。

MWE Gold V4系列是该设计理念的最新迭代，其内在品质远超中端定位给人的预期。该平台由Gospower与酷冷至尊联合开发，目前为酷冷至尊独占，短期内你不会在其他品牌上见到相同设计。该系列完全符合ATX 3.1和PCIe 5.1规范，配备原生12V-2x6连接器，并引入了酷冷至尊正在申请专利的GPU Shield电流监测功能，同时PFC和LLC级均采用数字控制方案。本次评测的750W型号瞄准主流游戏市场的核心需求，享有十年质保，建议零售价119美元。

**规格与设计**

该电源采用紧凑的ATX尺寸（140×150×86 mm），是目前最浅的现代ATX电源之一，能轻松适配任何兼容ATX电源的机箱，并为线缆管理预留充足空间。风扇格栅采用菱形镶嵌图案，由四颗裸露螺丝固定，侧板上有金色细条纹和金色MWE标志，其余均为黑色外壳。工艺质感平滑悦目，但与整机丰富特性清单所暗示的档次相比略显普通。

后部配备交流电插座和凹陷式主开关，周围有大量开孔用于散热。没有半被动风扇模式的开关——零转速行为是自动且不可用户选择的，这可能会让部分装机者感到不便。前部是全模组连接器面板，丝印清晰，设有共享8针插座组（用于PCIe和CPU线缆）、原生12V-2x6插座以及两者之间的一颗小型指示灯。

这颗小LED灯就是GPU Shield的可见界面——这是酷冷至尊正在申请专利的电流管理功能，也是V4系列的市场核心。该电路实时监测流经12V-2x6连接器各针脚的电流，如果检测到任何针脚出现异常电流（典型迹象为连接器未插紧或损坏），它会介入并通过LED指示故障。鉴于近年来连接器熔化问题频发，在119美元的电源上配置针脚级看门狗功能，绝非噱头，而是一个真正实用的防御措施——尽管我们希望大多数用户永远不需要看到LED亮起。

**内部设计**

散热采用120mm的鸿华HA1225H12F-Z风扇，油压轴承（HDB）型号，额定2400 RPM，0.58A。鸿华在入门到中端电源中很常见，虽然不如高端日本风扇有吸引力，但HDB变体在此级别是合理耐用的选择，其最高转速为平台提供了充足的热余量。

如前所述，该平台由Gospower设计，与酷冷至尊合作生产并独家供应，因此“到底是谁做的”这个问题有了明确答案。内部布局简洁现代，功率输出端采用大型散热片，小电压轨使用垂直子板，大量灰色硅胶用于固定元件。瞬态滤波从交流电插座后的小型PCB开始，延续至主板，包含两个Y电容、两个X电容、两个滤波电感和一个MOV——基础但完整的滤波方案，无遗漏。一个继电器与常见的NTC热敏电阻共同处理浪涌电流旁路。两个桥式整流器安装在独立散热片上，为APFC级供电，其升压元件与初级逆变级MOSFET共享长散热片。主电容为单个590μF，来自知名日本制造商TK（东光兴产）。初级逆变级采用半桥LLC谐振拓扑，这是该级别成熟且经济的方案，酷冷至尊声称PFC和LLC级的数字调校相比上一代MWE可提升约三个百分点的效率——尽管最终产品仍停留在金牌效率等级，这一说法值得商榷。次级侧，12V轨由四个MOSFET通过同步整流在主PCB上生成，而3.3V和5V轨则来自垂直子板上的DC-DC转换器。次级滤波大量使用CapXon电容。

**冷测试结果（25°C环境）**

在25°C环境温度下，MWE Gold 750 V4的能效曲线完全符合其金牌认证的预期。在标称负载范围内，230V输入时平均效率90.4%，115V时89.3%，在50%负载附近达到峰值92.6%（230V），即使在持续750W满载下仍保持90.2%。曲线并非完全平坦，但满载下无显著下降。数值稳稳落在80Plus金牌区间。该单元目前尚未获得Cybenetics认证——截至撰写本文时，Cybenetics数据库中尚无MPM-75F4-AFG的记录。

声学方面，冷态运行表现极佳。零转速模式在负载低于约40%（约300W）时保持风扇完全停转，覆盖桌面工作和轻度游戏。风扇在大约40%负载时启动，此时仅发出31.6 dB(A)的极低噪音，随后缓慢上升，仅在80%负载后超过39 dB(A)，持续750W满载时为44.2 dB(A)。热性能无需担忧，满载时初级和次级散热片温度分别仅为64°C和66°C。

**热测试结果（约45°C环境）**

在约45°C的恒温箱内（略高于该电源自身40°C额定温度），效率下降约两个百分点，230V时平均88.4%，115V时87.4%，满载时分别为86.5%和85.2%。这种退化幅度在温差下属于正常，尤其考虑到此时单元已超出其额定工作范围。噪音方面，高温改变了电源的性格：风扇更早退出被动模式，持续攀升至满载时的47.8 dB(A)——这个音量肯定能被听到，但尚未达到紧凑型金牌电源在同一条件下的风洞级别。内部温度是需要关注的值：满载时初级散热片约104°C，次级约105°C。平台保持了稳定性，元件规格也提供了一定保障，但在45°C环境下持续满载运行显然不是此电源的设计目标，任何主流750W电源也不应如此。

**电源质量与结论**

电压调整率在同级别中相对扎实。在20%至100%负载范围内，12V轨变化0.9%，3.3V轨1.1%，5V轨1.4%。未能达到高端数字平台的1%以内水准，但每条轨道都保持紧凑，其中12V线（现代系统唯一真正重视的电压）控制在1%以内，表现不错。纹波抑制同样体现了诚实够用的特点：12V轨满载时峰值54mV，在针对性交叉负载测试中为50mV；5V和3.3V轨最高分别为32mV和30mV。相比ATX设计限值（12V为120mV，3.3V/5V为50mV），这些数值留有约55%的余量，虽然高端平台通常能将这些数字减半，但对于750W金牌电源实际驱动的元件来说，滤波已足够。

所有保护机制均通过测试并正确触发。在热态下，3.3V轨的OCP在134%跳闸，5V轨为136%，12V轨为128%（80A）；OPP在大约130%额定输出（约975W）时关闭电源。这些阈值对于主流电源来说偏宽泛，但并非出人意料。

**总结**

酷冷至尊MWE Gold 750 V4是一款定位精准的主流电源，它清楚地知道自己要成为什么。Gospower制造的平台——与酷冷至尊联合开发并独家提供——在关键方面保持现代：ATX 3.1合规、原生12V-2x6连接器、PFC和LLC级的数字控制，以及作为这个价位罕见新增功能的GPU Shield监测特性。工程基础明显优于MWE徽标的历史表现，而12V-2x6连接器保护功能也能吸引忧心忡忡的消费者。测试数据诚实符合认证水准：效率确实落在金牌区间且曲线可预测，80Plus认证真实可查。电压调整和纹波在所有轨道上均合规，12V线尤其稳定，尽管两项指标均不足以威胁高端市场。常温下的声学表现是一大亮点：真正的无风扇窗口覆盖300W负载，超出后噪音上升缓慢且克制。妥协之处也容易列举且大多数可以接受：电容几乎全部为台系（依赖CapXon聚合物电容——品质尚可，但十年保修需要承担一定压力）；单元在高温环境下持续重载时发出可闻噪音，内部温度也高于理想值。然而，上述问题在119美元价位上都不是真正的弱点——它们只是预算极限留下的印记。建议零售价119美元，实际零售价通常更低，性价比一目了然。市面上有更安静的、做工更好的、元件更高级的电源——但它们的价格都贵得多。对于围绕中端或中高端显卡的主流ATX 3.1装机需求，MWE Gold 750 V4提供了经过验证的金牌效率、当下最新的连接性、不同寻常的保护措施以及十年的质保，售价低于大多数竞品。这是一个易于推荐的选项，仅有少量保留意见。

history.title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
history.description:
A yard sign opposing a planned data center is displayed along Route 54 in Mount Carmel Township Northumberland County. | Image: Getty Images
